role: SurfaceNav is the host component that renders a cross-cutting navigation rail for switching between top-level surface overlays and falls back to plain buttons when the DNA design system is unavailable.
capabilities: Renders a vertical nav rail of surface tiles (map, decisions, fabric, board, sessions, transcript)
Injects and delegates to DNA.org.navRail when present
Provides degrade-clean fallback buttons if DNA navRail is missing or throws
Wires each tile click to open its corresponding surface via store call or window events
Applies stamped UI addresses to the nav container and each surface item
substance_why: The file implements real navigation wiring, a static surface registry, DNA integration with fallback rendering, and explicit event delegation, not just a stub.
interactions_present: Click/tap a nav tile to open its surface
Map tile dispatches an Escape keydown to dismiss overlays and return home
Fabric, board, sessions, and transcript tiles dispatch custom window events (channels:open, board:open, sessions:open, transcript:open)
DNA navRail click delegation via [data-nav] attribute
interactions_missing: No explicit keyboard arrow navigation or focus management for the DNA rail
No active/selected state indicating which surface is currently open
No ARIA current/pressed attributes on the active tile
No re-attempt to render DNA navRail if it loads after mount
No cleanup of injected DNA innerHTML on unmount
No visible decisions count pill in the nav itself
performance_risks: Direct host.innerHTML mutation on mount bypasses React reconciliation
Synthetic Escape keyboard event may trigger unrelated global Escape handlers
Event listener is attached to the injected DOM host rather than delegated from a stable React-rendered parent
reuse_why: Keep the surface registry and open-surface contract, but replace imperative DOM injection and window-event dispatch with idiomatic React composition and a proper design-system adapter.
decomposition_hints: Split the SURFACES registry and open-surface actions into a separate surface model/hook
Split the DNA navRail adapter into its own component that accepts items and an onNavigate callback
Split the fallback button list into a presentational React component
Separate the stamped address logic into a reusable utility if not already shared
